scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify the main seed flow with non-default penalties and penalties dict override, including an unknown key.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function correctly applies penalties to each added reaction, with db_penalty, ex_penalty, tp_penalty defaults overridden by penalties dict.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Weights must include all db, ex, tp reactions with correct penalties, with overrides from penalties dict even for keys not in added reactions.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Solution may miss reactions, wrong penalty values, or not handle penalties dict keys that don't correspond to added reactions.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
extended_model, weights = create_extended_model(
    model,
    db_penalty=5,
    ex_penalty=2,
    tp_penalty=3,
    penalties={"c1_db_rxn": 10}
)
result = weights
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new anti-shortcut robustness requirement introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must include at least one variant test case with different model configuration.
Scaled executable cases must provide stronger executable verification coverage for this operator.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test edge case with empty compartments and boundaries, all penalty parameters defaulting to 1, no penalties dict.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function correctly handles empty compartments and boundaries, applying default penalty of 1 to the only exchange reaction.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When compartments list is empty, db_added is empty; when boundaries list is empty, tp_added is empty; ex_added still present; all unspecified penalties default to 1.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Solution may assume at least one compartment or boundary, or fail to default penalties to 1.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new anti-shortcut robustness requirement introduced by this operator.
